prompt: Design a multi-stage alert escalation: when an API returns 500 errors, first notify the team channel after 1 minute. If unacknowledged after 5 minutes, page the on-call. If still unacknowledged after 15 minutes, page the engineering manager. Each stage should include progressively more diagnostic info.

Create an event-driven notification system for our data pipeline: when a dataset finishes processing, notify downstream consumers via webhook. If processing fails, notify the data team with the error details. If processing takes longer than 2x the historical average, notify without blocking.

We need a chatbot that responds to specific patterns in our #ask-engineering Slack channel. When someone asks about deploy freezes, it should reply with the current freeze schedule. When someone asks about on-call, it should reply with the current rotation. Handle ambiguous queries gracefully.

Design a trigger system for our smart home: when the last person leaves (all phones disconnect from Wi-Fi), wait 5 minutes, then lock all doors, arm the security system, turn off non-essential lights, and lower the thermostat. If someone returns within the 5-minute window, cancel everything.

Build a compliance notification system: when any production database query takes longer than 30 seconds, log it to the audit trail. When a query accesses PII tables, additionally notify the security team. When both conditions are true, trigger an immediate review request.

Create a trigger-response for our SaaS billing: when a payment fails, send the customer an email with retry instructions. If it fails again after 3 days, downgrade their plan. If they were a paying customer for >1 year, route to the retention team instead of auto-downgrading.

Design an automated standup bot for distributed teams across 3 time zones. At each zone's 9am, prompt team members for their update. Compile all responses into a digest posted at noon UTC. Handle missing responses with a gentle reminder, then mark as 'no update' after 2 hours.

We have IoT temperature sensors in a warehouse. When any zone exceeds 85°F, alert the facility manager. When 3+ zones exceed the threshold simultaneously, escalate to the regional manager. When temperature has been above threshold for 30+ minutes, trigger the emergency ventilation protocol.
